title: Market Brief — Saturday, March 28, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 24% (67/283), H200 at 24% (90/372), and B200 at 29% (97/339), all below the 25% threshold signaling tight supply. B200 spot surged 17.6% overnight to $9.19/hr, the largest single-day move in the stack, suggesting acute demand for next-gen silicon even as older chips hold steady.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 24% (67/283), H200 at 24% (90/372), and B200 at 29% (97/339), all below the 25% threshold signaling tight supply. B200 spot surged 17.6% overnight to $9.19/hr, the largest single-day move in the stack, suggesting acute demand for next-gen silicon even as older chips hold steady.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs $5.85/M on H100, $5.56/M on H200, and $8.40/M on B200. Medium-tier API providers selling at $3/M output are bleeding roughly $2.85/M tokens on H100 alone. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is either running on subsidized Chinese compute or burning cash at industrial scale. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when Claude Opus 4.6 delivers frontier capability at $25/M. DeepSeek V3.2 and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at sub-$0.60/M output are the undervalued trade, offering viable inference at prices no closed-source provider can match without margin destruction.
bullets: 24% of H100 GPUs are available, with only 67 of 283 units free on spot markets.
29% B200 availability (97/339) masks a 17.6% overnight price spike to $9.19/hr median.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 undercuts the nearest competitor by 32%.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$8.40/M is the TCO-adjusted B200 inference cost, making mid-tier API pricing unprofitable on new silicon.
0.6 cents per million output tokens from Llama 4 Maverick is 133x cheaper than OpenAI o3 Pro.
